docs.title: WhisperJAV
docs.text:
Japanese Adult Video Subtitle Generator with AI-powered transcription

WhisperJAV is an open-source tool that generates accurate Japanese subtitles from video and audio files using OpenAI Whisper and other speech recognition models, with custom enhancements for Japanese language processing.

docs.title: 1. Install
docs.text:
Windows (Installer)Windows (Python)macOSLinux

Download WhisperJAV-x.x.x-Windows-x86_64.exe from the latest release. Run it — no admin rights required.

Full Windows installer guide

git clone https://github.com/meizhong986/whisperjav.git
cd whisperjav
installer\install_windows.bat

Full Python install guide

git clone https://github.com/meizhong986/whisperjav.git
cd whisperjav
python3 -m venv ~/venvs/whisperjav
source ~/venvs/whisperjav/bin/activate
./installer/install_mac.sh

Full macOS guide

git clone https://github.com/meizhong986/whisperjav.git
cd whisperjav
./installer/install_linux.sh

Full Linux guide

docs.title: 2. Launch the GUI
docs.text:
whisperjav-gui

Or use the Desktop shortcut (Windows installer).

docs.title: 3. Transcribe
docs.text:
1. Drag a video onto the app
  2. Click Start
  3. SRT subtitle file appears next to your video

That's it. For more control, see the GUI User Guide.

docs.title: Features at a Glance
docs.text: Feature Description Multiple pipelines Balanced, Fast, Faster, Fidelity, Transformers — trade speed for accuracy Ensemble mode Run two passes with different backends, merge for best results ChronosJAV Dedicated pipeline with anime-whisper and Kotoba models for anime/JAV content Qwen3-ASR Alternative ASR engine with strong Japanese performance AI Translation Translate subtitles using DeepSeek, Gemini, Claude, GPT, or local LLMs Speech enhancement ClearVoice denoising, BS-RoFormer vocal isolation, FFmpeg DSP chain Scene detection Auditok, Silero, Semantic — split long audio for better accuracy GUI + CLI Full-featured GUI for interactive use, CLI for automation and scripting

docs.title: WhisperJAV Issue Tracker
docs.text: Last updated: 2026-02-27 (v1.8.5-hotfix2 RELEASED — merged to main, tagged, wheel + exe built) Open issues: 49 | Open discussions: 10

docs.title: Methodology
docs.text: For every issue below: - "Fixed" means we have a commit that addresses the reported problem, but the reporter has NOT confirmed it works for them yet. The correct action is to comment on the issue with the fix details and ask for retest — not to close. - "Verified fixed" means the reporter (or another affected user) confirmed resolution. Safe to close. - "Partially addressed" means our work covers some but not all of what the user reported. - "Retest FAILED" means the reporter tested our fix and it did NOT resolve their issue. Needs re-investigation. - Attachments marked MANUAL need you to download from GitHub and review — they are file links I cannot access.

docs.title: #189 — Ensemble Smart Merge clears content of one pass ✅ FIXED
docs.text:
• Reporter: Ywocp | Created: Feb 27 | Status: Fixed in hotfix2 (d00bfb8)
  • Category: Ensemble/Pipeline
  • Fix: Quality-aware text tiebreaker for one-pass subsumption. Commit d00bfb8.

docs.title: #190 — Local LLM GBK codec error during Chinese translation 🆕
docs.text:
• Reporter: MatteoHugo | Created: Feb 27 | Status: Open (NEW)
  • Category: Translation/Encoding
  • Analysis: 'gbk' codec can't decode byte 0x80 during local LLM translation to Chinese. Translation interrupts at ~30 minutes, only partial output (~30min of ~2hr video). English target language works fine. Same encoding family as #186/#177 (cp950/GBK vs UTF-8) but in the local LLM code path. Hotfix1 (8fe643a) added UTF-8 encoding to 13 subprocess calls in llama_build_utils.py and local_backend.py, but may have missed a call path, or the error originates in PySubtrans's local client reading the LLM server's non-UTF-8 stdout.
  • Action: Investigate which subprocess/pipe call is using the system default codec (GBK on Simplified Chinese Windows). Likely a missed call in the local server ↔ PySubtrans boundary, or a file write in the translation output path.

docs.title: #188 — Unknown translation provider: Gemini ✅ FIXED (user action needed)
docs.text:
• Reporter: HawaiianPizza | Created: Feb 26 | Status: Fixed in hotfix2 (8d0deae)
  • Category: Translation/Provider
  • Root cause: NOT a casing bug. 'Gemini' is the correct PySubtrans provider name. PySubtrans silently skips provider registration when google-genai SDK is missing — the GeminiProvider class is never defined, so the provider isn't registered. User gets cryptic "Unknown translation provider" error.
  • Fix: Added actionable error message with pip install google-genai hint. Commit 8d0deae.
  • User action: pip install google-genai or pip install "whisperjav[translate]".

docs.title: #187 — v1.8.5 cannot generate subtitles ✅ FIXED (dup of #185)
docs.text:
• Reporter: weifu8435 | Created: Feb 26 | Status: Fixed in hotfix2 (5b6d79e)
  • Category: Runtime regression
  • Fix: Same root cause as #185/#173 — GUI passes None/string VAD params. Factory-level validation gate. Commit 5b6d79e.

docs.title: #186 — UnicodeEncodeError when running whisperjav in subprocess
docs.text:
• Reporter: teijiIshida | Created: Feb 24 | Status: Fixed in v1.8.5-hotfix1, awaiting retest
  • Category: Encoding
  • Attachments: MANUAL — whisperjav log.txt is a GitHub file attachment link, not inline text. Need to download and review.
  • Analysis: User embeds whisperjav in a Python subprocess and gets UnicodeEncodeError. This is the same encoding family as #177 (cp950 codec). Root cause: Windows console codepage is not UTF-8, and our subprocess Popen / file I/O doesn't enforce encoding='utf-8'.
  • Fix: Commit 8fe643a — Added encoding='utf-8', errors='replace' to 13 subprocess.run() calls across llama_build_utils.py (6 calls) and local_backend.py (7 calls).
  • Action: Awaiting retest from reporter.

docs.title: #185 — v1.8.4+ fails to run (v1.8.0/1.8.3 work fine) ✅ FIXED
docs.text:
• Reporter: weifu8435 | Created: Feb 22 | Status: Fixed in hotfix2 (5b6d79e)
  • Category: Runtime regression
  • Root cause: Same as #173 — GUI "Customize Parameters" passes None/string values to VAD backend constructors. v1.8.5's inline #173 fix only covered TEN VAD; the broader pattern (all backends, all param types) wasn't addressed.
  • Fix: Factory-level parameter validation gate for ALL speech segmenters. Sanitizes None→default, string→int/float for all backends before they reach backend code. Commit 5b6d79e.

docs.title: #183 — Ubuntu installation: llama_build_utils + local LLM token limit
docs.text:
• Reporter: destinyawaits | Created: Feb 22 | Status: Open (two separate issues)
  • Category: Installer/Linux + Translation/Local
  • Attachments: OK — full inline logs for both issues.
  • Analysis: Two distinct problems in one report:
  • llama_build_utils relative import error during install_linux.sh --local-llm. Same as #158. This is a regression from the installer refactor — the module can't be imported standalone because it uses relative imports. Still broken in v1.8.5.
  • Local LLM translation fails with "Hit API token limit, retrying batch without context" then regex parse failure. This is a PySubtrans issue — the model's response doesn't match expected #N Translation> format after context is stripped. Could be model-specific or batch-size related. Not our code per se, but we should provide better guidance (reduce batch size, try different model).
  • User also provides corrected Ubuntu GUI prerequisites (gir1.2-girepository-2.0, etc.) which are useful for docs.
  • Action: (1) Fix llama_build_utils import for Linux. (2) Consider adding guidance on token limit errors. (3) Update Linux install docs with GUI deps.

docs.title: #178 — Custom endpoint URL ignored (always connects to OpenAI) ⚠️ UPSTREAM BUG
docs.text:
• Reporter: Ywocp | Created: Feb 21 | Status: Hotfix2 partial fix + upstream PySubtrans bug identified
  • Category: Translation/GUI
  • Attachments: OK — full repro steps, detailed retest report with 3 scenarios.
  • Analysis (ORIGINAL): Endpoint passthrough was missing from translate-during-transcription code path.
  • Fix (hotfix1): Commit 8fe643a — Added --translate-endpoint CLI arg, passed to all call sites.
  • Retest Result (2026-02-27, Ywocp): ALL THREE test scenarios FAILED with 404 on /v4/responses.
  • Root cause (VERIFIED in hotfix2): Two layers:
  • (A) URL normalization (our bug, FIXED in hotfix2): Users pasting full endpoint URLs like https://api.example.com/v1/chat/completions caused doubled suffix since OpenAI SDK appends /chat/completions automatically. Fixed: _normalize_api_base() strips trailing API path suffixes in all 3 code paths (core.py, cli.py, service.py).
  • (B) PySubtrans upstream bug (NOT ours to fix): Provider_OpenAI.model_is_reasoning_model() treats every non-GPT model as "reasoning" → routes to OpenAIReasoningClient → client.responses.create() → /responses endpoint. Third-party providers only implement /chat/completions. This causes 404 for ALL non-GPT models (GLM, Qwen, DeepSeek, etc.) on any endpoint.
  • Hotfix2 fix: Commit 8d0deae — URL normalization in core.py, cli.py, service.py. Comment posted to #178 explaining upstream bug.
  • Impact: MEDIUM — URL normalization helps, but upstream PySubtrans bug still blocks non-GPT models on custom endpoints.
  • Action: Upstream bug report to machinewrapped/llm-subtrans pending (user will file). WhisperJAV cannot monkey-patch PySubtrans internals — that's unmaintainable.

docs.title: #177 — AI translation cp950 codec error
docs.text:
• Reporter: stonecfc | Created: Feb 20 | Status: Fixed in v1.8.5-hotfix1, awaiting retest
  • Category: Encoding
  • Attachments: OK — inline error log.
  • Analysis: 'cp950' codec can't decode byte 0xe6 in position 28 — this is Traditional Chinese Windows codepage encountering UTF-8 bytes. Same root cause family as #186.
  • Fix: Commit 8fe643a — Same fix as #186. Added encoding='utf-8', errors='replace' to all 13 subprocess.run() calls in translate module.
  • Action: Awaiting retest from reporter.

docs.title: #176 — Translation settings lost after GUI restart ✅ FIXED (hotfix2)
docs.text:
• Reporter: Ywocp | Created: Feb 20 | Status: Fixed in hotfix2, awaiting retest
  • Category: GUI/Persistence
  • Attachments: OK — detailed retest report.
  • Analysis (ORIGINAL): PyWebView private_mode=True causes ephemeral localStorage.
  • Fix (hotfix1): Commit 8fe643a — Set private_mode=False with storage_path. INSUFFICIENT — WebView2 localStorage unreliable between sessions.
  • Root cause (VERIFIED in hotfix2): The JS TranslationSettingsModal only saved to localStorage and never called the backend file persistence API (settings.json). WebView2's localStorage is unreliable across sessions regardless of private_mode setting.
  • Fix (hotfix2): Commit 8d0deae — Dual persistence architecture:
  • api.py: New get_translation_settings() / save_translation_settings() bridge methods with camelCase↔snake_case key mapping. Merges with existing settings to preserve fields the modal doesn't manage.
  • app.js: save() now async — persists to both localStorage (fast sync cache) and backend file (authoritative). New loadSettingsFromBackend() loads from backend file on pywebviewready event (after WebView2 bridge is available).
  • Timing: localStorage loaded sync during DOMContentLoaded, backend file loaded async during pywebviewready (overrides with authoritative data).
  • Impact: MEDIUM — translation settings now persist to ~/.config/WhisperJAV/translate/settings.json.
  • Action: Awaiting retest from reporter.

docs.title: #174 — GUI pipeline/ensemble settings reset every close
docs.text:
• Reporter: wazzur1 | Created: Feb 19 | Status: Open
  • Category: GUI/Persistence
  • Attachments: OK — short description.
  • Analysis: Unlike #176, the pipeline/ensemble settings have ZERO persistence code. There are no localStorage calls for pass1/pass2 pipeline, model, sensitivity, segmenter, etc. The EnsembleManager.init() just reads HTML default values. This is a larger feature (serialize ~30+ form fields, handle version migration for changed defaults, need reset-to-defaults). Fixing #176 (WebView2 storage_path) is a prerequisite but doesn't solve this.
  • Action: Defer to dedicated design phase (v1.9.0). #176 must work first.

docs.title: #167 — Qwen process hangs after "Saved artifacts"
docs.text:
• Reporter: kyze1010 | Created: Feb 14 | Status: Fix committed, NOT verified by user
  • Category: Qwen/Pipeline
  • Attachments: OK — inline debug logs.
  • Analysis: We committed fix 7bf0afc (prevent double log output causing subprocess pipe hang). However: (1) the reporter retested on v1.8.4 and it STILL hung, (2) meizhong986's last comment says he's downloading ENKI-085 to test himself. The fix in v1.8.5 may help but we have NO confirmation. The reporter also noted not all videos fail — suggesting it may be content-dependent (possibly qwen3-asr model glitch on certain audio).
  • Action: Comment with v1.8.5 release info and ask for retest. Do NOT close until confirmed.

docs.title: #163 — Subtitle sync issue / stuck on 3050/4G ⚠️ UPDATED
docs.text:
• Reporter: hosmallming | Created: Feb 11 | Status: Hardware limitation identified
  • Category: Timing + Performance
  • Attachments: OK — config description + screenshots.
  • Analysis (UPDATED 2026-02-27): User attempted v1.8.5 locally but got stuck at scene 2/18 for 5+ hours on RTX 3050/4GB. This is a VRAM limitation, not a timing bug. User is reverting to Kaggle. The original sync issue (subtitles ahead of audio) remains untested on v1.8.5 due to the VRAM constraint.
  • Impact: LOW — hardware-specific limitation, not a code bug.
  • Action: Acknowledge hardware limitation. The sync issue will need to be tested by another user or on Kaggle when notebook is updated.

docs.title: #125 — Error code 3221226505 (0xC0000409 crash)
docs.text:
• Reporter: emike99 | Created: Jan 11 | Status: Open, NOT fixed
  • Category: Runtime/Crash
  • Attachments: OK — embedded screenshot + several users posted logs in comments.
  • Analysis: Multiple reporters with varying hardware (4GB to 128GB RAM, various GPUs). Error code 0xC0000409 = STATUS_STACK_BUFFER_OVERRUN — a Windows structured exception in a native library (torch, CTranslate2, etc.) during cleanup. NOT OOM. NOT fixed.
  • Action: Keep open. Needs debug logs from affected users with v1.8.5.

docs.title: #179 — Ensemble: per-video processing order
docs.text:
• Reporter: yangming2027 | Created: Feb 21
  • Category: Ensemble/Architecture
  • Analysis: User runs 8 videos overnight. Current order: all pass1 → all pass2 → all merge. If PC crashes during pass1 phase, no completed subtitles exist. Wants: video1 (pass1→pass2→merge) → video2 → etc. A second user (jl6564) supports this request. This is a valid architectural change to the ensemble orchestrator's file iteration order.
  • Priority: Medium. High user impact for overnight batch users.

docs.title: #159 — CLI: pass specific numeric VAD threshold
docs.text:
• Reporter: SingingDalong | Created: Feb 3
  • Category: CLI/Feature request
  • Analysis: User wants --vad-threshold 0.6 for Whisper pipelines (balanced/fast/faster/fidelity), not Qwen. They're on Kaggle/Colab. We added --qwen-vad-threshold in v1.8.5 — but that only works for the Qwen pipeline. The Whisper pipelines still only accept preset strings via --sensitivity. This feature request is NOT addressed.
  • Priority: Medium. Proper fix: add --vad-threshold and --vad-padding as generic CLI args that work across all pipelines, or expose per-segmenter overrides.

docs.title: #172 — Ranking AI translation providers
docs.text:
• Reporter: teijiIshida | Created: Feb 19
  • Analysis: Community question. One user shared experience preferring DeepSeek for speed. Could be answered with testing data or left as community discussion.
  • Action: Answer or leave as discussion.

docs.title: #170 — Best dual-channel accuracy config?
docs.text:
• Reporter: weifu8435 | Created: Feb 19
  • Analysis: User asking for recommended ensemble settings. Community member (justantopair-ai) provided guidance. User has follow-up questions about Silero versions and Qwen. The v1.8.5 defaults (silero v6.2, vad-grouped framer, off regroup) would be relevant here.
  • Action: Answer with v1.8.5 recommended defaults.

docs.text:
• Reporter: teh-kogorta | Created: Feb 15
  • Analysis: Title says "SOLVED". User found that the non-parallel notebook works while the parallel one doesn't. meizhong986 acknowledged the parallel notebook is fragile.
  • Action: Can close since user self-resolved. But parallel notebook still needs update.

docs.title: #160 — Backend performance / "Maximum accuracy" rationale
docs.text:
• Reporter: mr-cn | Created: Feb 3
  • Analysis: Thoughtful question about why fidelity uses openai-whisper vs faster-whisper. Asks about stable-ts post-processing advantages. No response yet.
  • Action: Worth answering — good community engagement.

docs.text:

docs.title: #76 — L4 GPU Colab config
docs.text:
• Reporter: vnhung1908 | Created: Dec 11
  • Analysis: meizhong986 explained that most processing is CPU-bound. L4 vs T4 shows minimal difference because GPU isn't the bottleneck for audio preprocessing. Good discussion in comments.
  • Action: Informational. Can close with summary.

docs.title: #103 — Disk space after uninstall
docs.text:
• Reporter: trimine | Created: Jan 2
  • Analysis: User asked about residual disk usage after uninstall. meizhong986 explained: ~/.cache/huggingface/ (~3GB models), %LOCALAPPDATA%/.whisperjav_cache. User replied "Problem solved, thanks!"
  • Action: User confirmed resolved. Safe to close.

docs.title: D#101 — LLM prompt slot for target language
docs.text:
• Author: yslin1995 | Created: Jan 2
  • Status: Fixed in v1.8.5-hotfix1
  • Fix: Commit e783723 — Updated both gist instruction files (standard + pornify) to be language-neutral. Removed all hardcoded "English" references. Switched gist URLs from pinned-commit to latest format so future edits propagate immediately. Bundled defaults were already language-neutral. Target language is communicated through PySubtrans target_language parameter and [ to language] template syntax.

docs.text:
• Author: hosmallming | Created: Jan 9

docs.text:
• Author: stephanhixen | Created: Jan 15

docs.title: Hotfix1 Retest Failure Analysis (resolved in hotfix2)
docs.text:
Two hotfix1 fixes were confirmed NOT working by reporter Ywocp (2026-02-27). Both re-investigated and addressed in hotfix2:

  1. #178 (endpoint passthrough): Hotfix2 added URL normalization to prevent doubled API path suffixes. However, the core 404 issue is an upstream PySubtrans bug — model_is_reasoning_model() misclassifies all non-GPT models as "reasoning", routing them to /responses instead of /chat/completions. This cannot be fixed by WhisperJAV without unmaintainable monkey-patching.

  2. #176 (settings persistence): Hotfix2 bypassed unreliable WebView2 localStorage entirely. New dual persistence: JS saves to both localStorage (sync cache) and backend settings.json (authoritative). Backend loads override localStorage on pywebviewready.

docs.title: #190 — GBK Codec Error — FIXED in v1.8.6 dev (commit 68d1644)
docs.text:
Status: ~~User MatteoHugo confirmed on 2026-03-02 that the error persisted after hotfix3.~~ Now fixed: commit 68d1644 forces UTF-8 mode for the entire Python process on non-UTF-8 Windows systems. This sets PYTHONIOENCODING=utf-8 process-wide, which covers both WhisperJAV's own subprocess calls AND PySubtrans's internal encoding. Awaiting user verification.

Previous status: Hotfix3 only fixed WhisperJAV's subprocess encoding. The error originated in PySubtrans's own I/O path. The new fix covers the entire process tree.

docs.title: #183 — Ubuntu LLM Translation Fails (Secondary) — FIXED in v1.8.6 dev (commit 24faa3b)
docs.text: Status: ~~Translation failed with "API token limit" + "No matches".~~ Now fixed: commit 24faa3b auto-caps batch size when the local LLM's context window is small. The root cause was PySubtrans sending batches larger than the model's context could handle. The fix detects the model's max context and reduces batch_size accordingly. Also: the upgrade tool now works on Linux (commit 2e402f3).

CLI batch workflow #49, #51, #126 #49/#51 for v1.8.6. #126 deferred. Translation provider ~~#178~~, ~~#188~~, ~~#143~~, ~~#150~~, #69 Most fixed. #69 covered by custom provider.

docs.title: GitHub Discussions (10 open)
docs.text:
# Title Notes D#24 Welcome Pinned D#101 LLM prompt slot suggestion Instruction prompts shipped in hotfix1 D#121 Kaggle support suggestion Kaggle notebook shipped D#131 Local Models thank you Custom provider covers this D#133 --instructions-file override? Documentation question D#145 Local LLM discussion General discussion D#147 AMD GPU discussion Deferred D#154 Initial prompt to reduce hallucination Feature consideration D#156 --max-workers CPU flag Documentation question D#182 Can't find whisperjav in folder Installation support

This tracker is a point-in-time analysis. For live status, see GitHub Issues.

docs.title: Manual Rollback Guide
docs.text: If an upgrade fails or causes issues, follow these steps to restore WhisperJAV to a working state.

docs.title: Quick Reference
docs.text: Scenario Solution Upgrade failed mid-way Option A: Reinstall specific version New version has bugs Option A: Reinstall specific version Complete corruption Option B: Fresh install Can't run Python at all Option B: Fresh install

docs.title: Option A: Reinstall Specific Version
docs.text: Use this if Python still works but WhisperJAV is broken.

docs.title: Step 1: Open Command Prompt
docs.text:
1. Press Win + R
  2. Type cmd and press Enter

docs.title: Step 2: Navigate to WhisperJAV
docs.text: cd %LOCALAPPDATA%\WhisperJAV

docs.title: Step 3: Reinstall the version you want
docs.text:
To reinstall latest stable: 

python.exe -m pip install --force-reinstall git+https://github.com/meizhong986/whisperjav.git@main

To install a specific version (e.g., v1.7.3): 

python.exe -m pip install --force-reinstall git+https://github.com/meizhong986/whisperjav.git@v1.7.3

Available version tags: - v1.7.4 - Latest - v1.7.3 - Previous stable - v1.7.2, v1.7.1, v1.7.0 - Older versions

docs.title: Step 4: Verify installation
docs.text: python.exe -c "from whisperjav import __version__; print(__version__)"

docs.title: Option B: Fresh Install
docs.text: Use this if the Python environment is completely broken.

docs.title: Step 1: Uninstall WhisperJAV
docs.text:
1. Open Settings → Apps → Apps & features
  2. Search for "WhisperJAV"
  3. Click Uninstall

Or manually delete: 

rmdir /s /q "%LOCALAPPDATA%\WhisperJAV"

docs.title: Step 2: Download fresh installer
docs.text: Go to WhisperJAV Releases and download the latest .exe installer.

docs.title: Step 3: Run the installer
docs.text: The installer will create a fresh environment with all dependencies.

docs.title: Preserving Your Data
docs.text:
Your AI models and cache are stored separately and survive reinstallation:

Data Location Preserved? Whisper models ~/.cache/whisper/ Yes HuggingFace models ~/.cache/huggingface/ Yes WhisperJAV config %LOCALAPPDATA%\WhisperJAV\whisperjav_config.json Deleted on uninstall

To preserve config before uninstall: 

copy "%LOCALAPPDATA%\WhisperJAV\whisperjav_config.json" "%USERPROFILE%\Desktop\"

docs.title: "pip is not recognized"
docs.text:
Your PATH is not set correctly. Use the full path: 

%LOCALAPPDATA%\WhisperJAV\python.exe -m pip install ...

docs.title: "Permission denied"
docs.text: Close all WhisperJAV windows and try again. If still failing: 1. Open Task Manager (Ctrl+Shift+Esc) 2. End any python.exe or pythonw.exe processes from WhisperJAV 3. Try the command again

docs.title: "Network error"
docs.text:
Check your internet connection. If behind a proxy: 

set HTTPS_PROXY=http://your-proxy:port
python.exe -m pip install ...

docs.title: Getting Help
docs.text:
If none of the above works:

  1. Open an issue at GitHub Issues
  2. Include:
  3. What you were doing when it broke
  4. Error messages (screenshot or copy-paste)
  5. Windows version
  6. Output of: python.exe --version

docs.title: WhisperJAV Upgrade Guide
docs.text: This guide covers how to upgrade WhisperJAV to the latest version.

docs.title: GUI Users (Recommended)
docs.text: The GUI will automatically check for updates on startup and display a notification banner when a new version is available.

docs.title: Update Notification Banner
docs.text:
When an update is available, you'll see a colored banner at the top of the window:

Color Update Type Description Red Critical Security or critical bug fix - always shown Blue Major Major version update (e.g., 1.7 → 2.0) Green Minor New features (e.g., 1.7.4 → 1.8.0) Gray Patch Bug fixes (e.g., 1.7.4 → 1.7.5)

docs.title: One-Click Update
docs.text:
1. Click Update Now on the notification banner
  2. Confirm the update in the dialog
  3. The GUI will close automatically
  4. The update runs in the background
  5. The GUI relaunches when complete

docs.title: View Release Notes
docs.text: Click Release Notes on the banner to see what's new in the update.

docs.title: Dismiss Notifications
docs.text: Click the × button to dismiss the notification. You'll be reminded: - Patch updates: Weekly - Minor updates: Monthly - Major updates: Every launch - Critical updates: Always shown (cannot dismiss)

docs.title: CLI Users
docs.text: Use the whisperjav-upgrade command for command-line upgrades.

docs.title: Check for Updates
docs.text:
whisperjav-upgrade --check

This shows if an update is available without installing it.

docs.title: Interactive Upgrade
docs.text:
whisperjav-upgrade

You'll be prompted to confirm before the upgrade begins.

docs.title: Non-Interactive Upgrade
docs.text:
whisperjav-upgrade --yes

Automatically confirms the upgrade (useful for scripts).

docs.title: Hot-Patch Mode
docs.text:
whisperjav-upgrade --wheel-only

Only updates the WhisperJAV package itself, skipping dependency installation. This is faster and safer for post-release patches (e.g., 1.7.4 → 1.7.4.post1).

docs.title: Show Script Version
docs.text: whisperjav-upgrade --version

docs.title: Force Update Check
docs.text:
whisperjav-upgrade --check --force

Bypasses the 6-hour cache to check for updates immediately.

docs.title: What Gets Updated
docs.text: Component Full Upgrade Wheel-Only WhisperJAV code Yes Yes New dependencies Yes No numpy/librosa fix Yes No Desktop shortcut Yes Yes Old files cleanup Yes No

docs.title: What Gets Preserved
docs.text: The upgrade preserves: - AI models (~/.cache/whisper/, ~/.cache/huggingface/) - Configuration (whisperjav_config.json) - Cache data (.whisperjav_cache/)

docs.title: Troubleshooting
docs.text: See UPGRADE_TROUBLESHOOTING.md for common issues and solutions.

docs.title: Manual Rollback
docs.text: If an upgrade fails, see MANUAL_ROLLBACK.md for recovery options.

docs.title: Running Standalone Upgrade Script
docs.text:
If the installed upgrade command doesn't work, you can download and run the standalone script:

cd %LOCALAPPDATA%\WhisperJAV
curl -O https://raw.githubusercontent.com/meizhong986/whisperjav/main/installer/upgrade_whisperjav.py
python.exe upgrade_whisperjav.py

docs.title: Version Check Caching
docs.text: Update checks are cached for 6 hours to avoid excessive API calls. Use --force to bypass the cache.

docs.title: Update Process Flow
docs.text:
1. GUI detects update → Shows notification banner
  2. User clicks "Update Now" → Confirmation dialog
  3. GUI spawns update wrapper → Background process
  4. GUI exits → Releases file locks
  5. Wrapper waits for GUI → Ensures clean exit
  6. Upgrade script runs → Updates packages
  7. Wrapper relaunches GUI → Fresh start

docs.title: Files Created
docs.text:
• update.log - Detailed upgrade log (in installation directory)
  • .whisperjav_cache/version_check.json - Cached update check result
  • .whisperjav_cache/update_dismissed.json - Dismissed notification tracking

docs.title: Upgrade Troubleshooting Guide
docs.text: This guide helps resolve common issues during WhisperJAV upgrades.

docs.title: "Insufficient disk space"
docs.text:
Error: Disk space: X.X GB available, need 5 GB

Solution: 1. Free up disk space on the drive where WhisperJAV is installed 2. Delete old files: %TEMP%\*, browser cache, old downloads 3. Run Disk Cleanup: cleanmgr

docs.title: "Wrong Python environment detected"
docs.text:
Error: 

Running from: C:\some\other\python
Expected:     C:\Users\...\AppData\Local\WhisperJAV

Solution: Run the upgrade script using WhisperJAV's Python: 

%LOCALAPPDATA%\WhisperJAV\python.exe upgrade_whisperjav.py

docs.title: "WhisperJAV GUI is running"
docs.text:
Error: GUI check failed: WhisperJAV GUI is running

Solution: 1. Close the WhisperJAV GUI window 2. If it doesn't close, open Task Manager and end: - pythonw.exe (WhisperJAV GUI) - WhisperJAV-GUI.exe 3. Try the upgrade again

docs.title: "No internet connection"
docs.text:
Error: Network: no connection to GitHub

Solution: 1. Check your internet connection 2. Try accessing https://github.com in a browser 3. If behind a corporate firewall/proxy: 

set HTTPS_PROXY=http://your-proxy:port
python.exe upgrade_whisperjav.py

docs.title: "Package upgrade failed"
docs.text:
Symptoms: Upgrade stops during "Upgrading WhisperJAV package..."

Causes: - Network timeout - GitHub temporarily unavailable - pip cache corruption

Solutions:

  1. Retry the upgrade: 

    python.exe upgrade_whisperjav.py

  2. Clear pip cache and retry: 

    python.exe -m pip cache purge
python.exe upgrade_whisperjav.py

  3. Use wheel-only mode (if you just need the latest code): 

    python.exe upgrade_whisperjav.py --wheel-only

docs.title: "Dependency installation failed"
docs.text:
Symptoms: Some packages fail to install during dependency phase

Solutions:

  1. Continue anyway - Most dependency failures are non-fatal
  2. Install manually: 
    python.exe -m pip install <package-name>

docs.title: "Permission denied" / "Access denied"
docs.text:
Symptoms: File operations fail

Solutions: 1. Close ALL WhisperJAV windows 2. Run upgrade from an elevated command prompt (Run as Administrator) 3. Temporarily disable antivirus real-time protection

docs.title: "Could not update launcher"
docs.text:
Symptoms: Desktop shortcut not updated

This is non-fatal. WhisperJAV still works. To fix manually: 1. Right-click the desktop shortcut 2. Click Properties 3. Update the target path

docs.title: "Import error" after upgrade
docs.text:
Symptoms: WhisperJAV won't start, shows import errors

Solutions:

  1. Reinstall with dependencies: 

    python.exe -m pip install --force-reinstall git+https://github.com/meizhong986/whisperjav.git@main

  2. Check for conflicting packages: 

    python.exe -m pip check

docs.title: "CUDA not available" after upgrade
docs.text:
Symptoms: GPU acceleration stopped working

Cause: CPU-only PyTorch was installed over CUDA version

Solution: 

REM Check your CUDA version first
nvidia-smi

REM Reinstall CUDA PyTorch (example for CUDA 12.1)
python.exe -m pip install torch torchaudio --index-url https://download.pytorch.org/whl/cu121 --force-reinstall

docs.title: Version didn't change after upgrade
docs.text:
Symptoms: --version shows old version

Solutions: 1. Clear Python's import cache: 

python.exe -c "import whisperjav; print(whisperjav.__file__)"
2. Reinstall: 
python.exe -m pip install --force-reinstall --no-cache-dir git+https://github.com/meizhong986/whisperjav.git@main

docs.title: Skip preflight checks
docs.text:
Use with caution - only if you know what you're doing: 

python.exe upgrade_whisperjav.py --skip-preflight

docs.title: Hot-patch mode
docs.text:
Only updates WhisperJAV code, skips all dependencies: 

python.exe upgrade_whisperjav.py --wheel-only

docs.title: Non-interactive mode
docs.text:
For scripted upgrades: 

python.exe upgrade_whisperjav.py --yes

docs.title: When All Else Fails
docs.text: See Manual Rollback Guide for complete recovery options.

docs.title: Reporting Issues
docs.text:
If you encounter an issue not covered here:

  1. Open GitHub Issues
  2. Include:
  3. Full console output from the upgrade attempt
  4. Windows version (winver)
  5. Python version (python.exe --version)
  6. Current WhisperJAV version (if known)

docs.title: WhisperJAV Multi-Track Visualization Tool
docs.text: Standalone utility for visualizing WhisperJAV processing outputs. Generates interactive HTML files with a DAW-style multi-track timeline showing 5 horizontal tracks.

docs.title: Track Details
docs.text: Track Description Visual Style Data Source 1. Waveform Audio amplitude envelope Blue filled area *_extracted.wav 2. Scene Pass 1 Coarse scene boundaries Olive/yellow blocks with labels *_master.json 3. Scene Pass 2 Fine scene boundaries Olive/yellow blocks with labels *_master.json 4. VAD Segments Speech regions (ASR preprocessing) Green blocks with "SPEECH" labels *_master.json 5. Subtitles Subtitle entries Purple blocks with timestamp + text *.srt

docs.title: Installation
docs.text:
The visualization tool has its own dependencies, separate from WhisperJAV:

pip install -r scripts/visualization/requirements.txt

Required packages: - plotly>=5.0.0 - Interactive charts - numpy>=1.20.0 - Array processing - librosa>=0.9.0 - Audio processing - pysrt>=1.1.2 - SRT parsing

docs.title: Prerequisites: Generating Data
docs.text:
Run WhisperJAV with the --keep-temp flag to preserve intermediate files:

whisperjav video.mp4 --mode balanced --keep-temp

This preserves: - temp/{basename}_extracted.wav - Extracted audio (16kHz mono) - temp/{basename}_master.json - Processing metadata with scene detection + VAD info - output/{basename}.{lang}.whisperjav.srt - Final subtitles

docs.title: Full Visualization (All Tracks)
docs.text:
python -m scripts.visualization.viz_cli \
    --audio ./temp/video_extracted.wav \
    --metadata ./temp/video_master.json \
    --srt ./output/video.ja.whisperjav.srt \
    --output ./output/video_viz.html

docs.title: Auto-Discovery Mode
docs.text:
Let the tool find audio and metadata automatically:

python -m scripts.visualization.viz_cli \
    --temp-dir ./temp \
    --basename video \
    --srt ./output/video.ja.whisperjav.srt \
    --output ./output/video_viz.html

docs.title: Metadata + SRT Only (No Waveform)
docs.text:
For faster generation without audio processing:

python -m scripts.visualization.viz_cli \
    --metadata ./temp/video_master.json \
    --srt ./output/video.ja.whisperjav.srt \
    --output ./output/video_viz.html

docs.title: Command-Line Options
docs.text: Option Description --audio, -a Path to extracted WAV file --metadata, -m Path to master metadata JSON --srt, -s Path to output SRT file (explicit path required) --temp-dir WhisperJAV temp directory (auto-discovers audio & metadata) --basename Media basename for auto-discovery --output, -o Output HTML file path (required) --title Custom visualization title --downsample-points Waveform detail level (default: 10000)

docs.title: Interactive Features
docs.text:
The generated HTML file includes:

  • Zoom/Pan: Use the range slider at the bottom or drag on the chart
  • Hover Tooltips: View detailed information about each block
  • Track Toggle: Click legend items to show/hide individual tracks
  • Scroll Zoom: Mouse scroll to zoom in/out
  • Export: Download chart as PNG via the toolbar

docs.title: Scene Detection Metadata
docs.text:
The *_master.json file contains scenes_detected with:

{
  "scenes_detected": [
    {
      "scene_index": 0,
      "start_time_seconds": 0.0,
      "end_time_seconds": 29.5,
      "duration_seconds": 29.5,
      "detection_pass": 1,
      "filename": "video_scene_0000.wav"
    }
  ]
}
  • detection_pass: 1 = Coarse (Pass 1) - shows on Track 2
  • detection_pass: 2 = Fine (Pass 2) - shows on Track 3

docs.title: VAD Segments (ASR Preprocessing)
docs.text:
VAD segments are captured from the ASR module's Silero VAD preprocessing:

{
  "vad_segments": [
    {"start_sec": 1.2, "end_sec": 5.8},
    {"start_sec": 10.5, "end_sec": 28.9}
  ],
  "vad_method": "silero",
  "vad_params": {
    "threshold": 0.08,
    "min_speech_duration_ms": 100
  }
}

These segments represent speech regions detected BEFORE transcription begins.

docs.title: Missing Plotly
docs.text:
ImportError: Plotly is required for visualization.

Solution: pip install plotly

docs.title: Empty Tracks
docs.text: If you see empty tracks: - Ensure --keep-temp was used when running WhisperJAV - Check that the metadata JSON exists and is valid - Verify file paths are correct - For VAD: Ensure you used --mode balanced (VAD is part of balanced pipeline)

docs.title: Slow Performance on Long Videos
docs.text:
For videos longer than 1 hour, reduce waveform detail level:

python -m scripts.visualization.viz_cli \
    --audio ./temp/long_video_extracted.wav \
    --downsample-points 5000 \
    --output ./output/viz.html

docs.title: Block Labels Not Showing
docs.text:
Labels only appear inside blocks that are wide enough: - Scene blocks: > 2 seconds - VAD blocks: > 1.5 seconds - Subtitle blocks: > 2 seconds

Zoom in using the range slider to see more detail.

docs.title: What video formats does WhisperJAV support?
docs.text: Any format FFmpeg can read: MP4, MKV, AVI, MOV, WMV, FLV, WAV, MP3, FLAC, M4A, M4B, and many more. If FFmpeg can extract audio from it, WhisperJAV can process it.

docs.title: How long does transcription take?
docs.text:
Depends on video length, pipeline, and GPU:

Video Length Faster (GPU) Balanced (GPU) Ensemble (GPU) Faster (CPU) 30 min ~1 min ~2 min ~4 min ~10 min 2 hours ~3 min ~5 min ~10 min ~40 min

These are rough estimates. Actual times vary with GPU model and audio complexity.

docs.title: Can I use WhisperJAV without a GPU?
docs.text: Yes. Go to Advanced → check "Accept CPU-only mode". Use Faster mode for best CPU speed. It works, just significantly slower.

docs.title: Which pipeline gives the best subtitles?
docs.text:
For anime/JAV content: ChronosJAV with anime-whisper, or Ensemble (Balanced + Qwen3-ASR with Smart Merge) for maximum accuracy.

For general Japanese content: Balanced mode is the best single-pass option.

docs.title: The subtitles have hallucinated text (random English phrases, URLs, etc.)
docs.text:
This is a known Whisper behavior on silent or very quiet sections. WhisperJAV includes a hallucination sanitizer that removes most of these. Try:

  1. Use Aggressive sensitivity (captures more speech, leaving less "silence" for hallucinations)
  2. Use Ensemble mode (two passes catch different hallucinations)
  3. Enable a Speech Enhancer (ClearVoice or BS-RoFormer) to clean the audio first

docs.title: The timing is off / subtitles appear too early or late
docs.text:
Try a different Scene Detector:

  • Semantic (default) — best for most content
  • Auditok — better for content with clear silence between dialogue
  • Silero — more aggressive splitting

Or try ChronosJAV pipeline, which uses TEN VAD for tighter timing.

docs.title: Which translation provider is best?
docs.text:
For cost-effectiveness: DeepSeek offers excellent quality at low cost, especially for CJK languages.

For quality: Claude or GPT-4 produce the most natural translations, but at higher cost.

For privacy: Local LLM runs entirely on your machine with no data leaving your computer.

docs.title: Translation fails with "API token limit" error
docs.text:
Your subtitle batches are too large for the model's context window. Try:

  1. Reduce Max Batch Size in Advanced Settings (try 15 or 10)
  2. Use a model with a larger context window
  3. WhisperJAV v1.8.6+ auto-caps batch size for local LLMs

docs.title: Translation shows "Unknown provider: Gemini" on Linux
docs.text: Install the missing dependency: pip install google-api-core. This is fixed in v1.8.6+.

docs.title: The installer is stuck / taking very long
docs.text: The first install downloads ~3-5 GB of packages. On slow connections this can take 30+ minutes. Check the install log in the installation directory for progress.

docs.title: "CUDA not available" but I have an NVIDIA GPU
docs.text:
1. Verify your NVIDIA driver version: nvidia-smi
  2. Driver 450+ required for CUDA 11.8, 570+ for CUDA 12.8
  3. You do NOT need to install the CUDA Toolkit — PyTorch bundles its own
  4. Try reinstalling PyTorch with the correct CUDA version

docs.title: How do I upgrade?
docs.text:
whisperjav-upgrade

Or for code-only (faster): whisperjav-upgrade --wheel-only

See the Upgrade Guide for details.

docs.title: Processing fails with an error
docs.text:
1. Check the Console at the bottom of the GUI for the error message
  2. Enable Debug logging in Advanced tab and retry — check whisperjav.log
  3. Try a simpler pipeline (Faster mode) to isolate the issue
  4. Report the error on GitHub Issues with the error message and your system details

docs.title: The GUI won't start
docs.text:
• Windows: Ensure WebView2 is installed (comes with Windows 10 1803+ and Windows 11)
  • Linux: Install libwebkit2gtk-4.0-dev (Ubuntu) or webkit2gtk4.0-devel (Fedora)
  • macOS: WebKit is built-in, should work automatically
  • Try launching from command line (whisperjav-gui) to see error output

docs.title: ADR-001: YAML-Driven Configuration Architecture (v4)
docs.text: Status: Accepted Date: 2024-11-29 Decision Makers: WhisperJAV Development Team

docs.title: Context
docs.text:
WhisperJAV needed a configuration system that could support:

  1. Multiple ASR ecosystems (Transformers, Kaldi, BERT) with different models
  2. GUI auto-generation from configuration schemas
  3. Patchability - update settings without rebuilding/redistributing the package
  4. Extensibility - add new models with minimal effort ("drop and go")
  5. Sensitivity presets across all components

The existing configuration systems (v1-v3) had limitations:

Version Approach Limitation v1 JSON config + Python classes Required Python changes to add models v2 Component registry Still Python-coupled, no GUI hints v3 Pydantic components Better validation, but still Python-first

docs.title: Key Question
docs.text:
"In which approach can I, as the main developer, update settings (parameter values) or add parameters, without needing to repackage and redistribute the entire product?"

Answer: Only a config-driven (YAML) approach allows patching without redistribution.

docs.title: Decision
docs.text: We adopt a YAML-first configuration architecture (v4) with the following characteristics:

docs.title: 1. YAML as Source of Truth
docs.text: All configuration lives in YAML files under whisperjav/config/v4/ecosystems/. Python code reads and validates YAML but does not define configuration structure.

docs.title: 2. Flat Dot-Notation Parameters
docs.text:
Parameters use flat keys with semantic prefixes: 

spec:
  model.id: "kotoba-tech/kotoba-whisper-v2.0"
  model.device: auto
  decode.beam_size: 5

This is easier to merge, patch, and display in GUIs than nested structures.

docs.title: 3. Schema Validation with Pydantic
docs.text: YAML files are validated against Pydantic models at load time. Invalid configurations fail fast with clear error messages.

docs.title: 4. GUI Hints in Configuration
docs.text:
gui:
  model.device:
    widget: dropdown
    options: [auto, cuda, cpu]

The GUI reads these hints to auto-generate configuration panels.

docs.title: 5. Lazy-Loading Registries
docs.text: Configurations are discovered and loaded on demand, with caching for performance.

docs.title: 6. Independence from Legacy
docs.text: The v4 system has zero dependencies on v1-v3 code. Both can coexist during migration.

docs.title: Alternative 1: Extend v3 Python Components
docs.text: Rejected because: - Still requires Python changes to add models - Cannot patch without redistribution - GUI hints would need to be added to Python classes

docs.title: Alternative 2: Hydra/OmegaConf
docs.text: Rejected because: - Heavy dependency for our use case - Learning curve for contributors - Overkill for single-application config

docs.title: Alternative 3: Pure JSON
docs.text: Rejected because: - No comments (harder to document inline) - More verbose than YAML - Less human-readable

docs.title: Positive
docs.text:
• Zero Python changes to add a model - just create a YAML file
  • Patchable - users can modify YAML without rebuilding
  • GUI auto-generation - widget hints in config enable dynamic UI
  • Clear separation - YAML defines "what", Python defines "how"
  • Version evolution - schemaVersion enables future changes

docs.title: Negative
docs.text:
• Two config systems - v4 coexists with legacy during transition
  • YAML learning curve - contributors need basic YAML knowledge
  • Potential drift - if legacy code isn't deprecated, patterns may diverge

docs.title: Mitigations
docs.text:
• Clear documentation in whisperjav/config/v4/README.md
  • Deprecation notices in legacy code and docs
  • This ADR for historical context

docs.title: Implementation
docs.text:
The v4 system is implemented in whisperjav/config/v4/ with:

Component Purpose schemas/ Pydantic models for YAML validation loaders/ YAML parsing with error handling registries/ Lazy-loading config discovery manager.py Central API for config access gui_api.py Frontend-friendly API ecosystems/ YAML config files

See whisperjav/config/v4/README.md for usage details.

docs.title: References
docs.text:
• Research: HuggingFace config patterns, Kubernetes strategic merge, Hydra composition
  • Domain Analysis: Identified entities (Ecosystem, Model, Tool, Preset) and relationships
  • Validation: Tested against use cases (add model, patch setting, GUI generation)

docs.title: ADR-002: Batched Transcription Pipeline Architecture
docs.text: Status: Proposed Date: 2024-12-11 Decision Makers: WhisperJAV Development Team Target Release: v1.8.0+

docs.title: Context
docs.text: WhisperJAV's current transcription pipeline processes scenes sequentially, where each scene goes through the full pipeline (speech segmentation → transcription → SRT) before the next scene begins.

docs.title: Current Architecture (Sequential)
docs.text:
Scene 1: Load → Speech Segmentation → Transcribe Groups → Write SRT
Scene 2: Load → Speech Segmentation → Transcribe Groups → Write SRT
Scene 3: Load → Speech Segmentation → Transcribe Groups → Write SRT
...
Final: Stitch all scene SRTs together

docs.title: Observations from Testing
docs.text: When processing a 5-minute audio file with 3 scenes: - Each scene triggers a separate speech segmentation pass - The Whisper model remains loaded but processes one VAD group at a time - Progress reporting shows scene-level updates, not segment-level - Total segments not known until all scenes are processed

docs.title: Key Question
docs.text: "Would a batched architecture—where all scenes are segmented first, then all segments transcribed together—provide better performance, progress reporting, and resource utilization?"

docs.title: Problem Statement
docs.text:
The sequential architecture has several limitations:

Issue Impact Repeated I/O Each scene loaded separately, potential cache misses No global progress Cannot show "X of Y total segments" until all scenes processed Memory fragmentation VAD model and Whisper model both in VRAM throughout Limited parallelization Scene processing is inherently serial Unpredictable ETA Cannot estimate completion without knowing total work

docs.title: Architecture Overview
docs.text:
Stage 1: Speech Segmentation (Batch All Scenes)
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ Scene 1 ─→ VAD ─→ [Group 1, Group 2]                           │
│ Scene 2 ─→ VAD ─→ [Group 1]                                    │
│ Scene 3 ─→ VAD ─→ [Group 1, Group 2, Group 3]                  │
│                                                                  │
│ Output: Global VAD Group Registry (6 groups total)              │
│         with provenance tracking (scene_idx, group_idx)         │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
                              │
                    [Release VAD model from VRAM]
                              │
                              ▼
Stage 2: Batched Transcription
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ Load Whisper model once                                         │
│                                                                  │
│ for group in global_registry:                                   │
│     transcribe(group.audio_data)                                │
│     track_provenance(group.scene_idx, group.group_idx)          │
│                                                                  │
│ Output: Transcription results with scene/group mapping          │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
                              │
                              ▼
Stage 3: SRT Reconstruction
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ Group results by scene_idx                                      │
│ Apply timestamp offsets per scene                               │
│ Write scene SRTs → Stitch → Final output                        │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

docs.title: Data Structures
docs.text:
@dataclass
class VADGroupEntry:
    """Entry in the global VAD group registry."""
    scene_idx: int           # Which scene this group belongs to
    group_idx: int           # Group index within the scene
    audio_data: np.ndarray   # Audio samples for this group
    sample_rate: int         # Audio sample rate
    start_sec: float         # Start time within scene
    end_sec: float           # End time within scene
    scene_offset_sec: float  # Scene start offset in original audio
    segments: List[SpeechSegment]  # Individual speech segments

class VADGroupRegistry:
    """Global registry of all VAD groups across all scenes."""
    entries: List[VADGroupEntry]

    @property
    def total_groups(self) -> int: ...

    @property
    def total_segments(self) -> int: ...

    def groups_by_scene(self, scene_idx: int) -> List[VADGroupEntry]: ...

docs.title: API Changes
docs.text:
# New method in BalancedPipeline
def process_batched(self, scene_paths: List[Tuple[Path, float, float, float]]) -> Dict:
    """Process all scenes using two-stage batched architecture."""

    # Stage 1: Batch speech segmentation
    registry = self._batch_segment_scenes(scene_paths)

    # Release VAD model
    self.asr.release_vad_model()

    # Stage 2: Batch transcription
    results = self.asr.transcribe_registry(registry)

    # Stage 3: Reconstruct SRTs
    return self._reconstruct_scene_srts(results, scene_paths)

# New method in FasterWhisperProASR
def transcribe_registry(self, registry: VADGroupRegistry) -> List[TranscriptionResult]:
    """Transcribe all groups in the registry."""
    ...

docs.title: Alternative 1: Keep Sequential, Add Caching
docs.text:
Approach: Cache VAD results, reuse if scene already processed.

Rejected because: - Still sequential processing - Caching adds complexity without addressing core issues - Progress reporting still scene-bound

docs.title: Alternative 2: Parallel Scene Processing
docs.text:
Approach: Process multiple scenes in parallel using multiprocessing.

Rejected because: - VRAM constraints limit parallelism (Whisper models are large) - Complexity of managing multiple GPU contexts - Diminishing returns on consumer hardware

docs.title: Alternative 3: Streaming Architecture
docs.text:
Approach: Stream audio through pipeline, process segments as they arrive.

Rejected because: - Major rewrite of pipeline architecture - Incompatible with scene detection paradigm - Overkill for batch processing use case

docs.title: Positive
docs.text: Benefit Description Global progress Know total segments upfront: "Transcribing 45/120 segments" Better ETA Accurate time estimates based on segments processed Memory optimization Release VAD model before loading Whisper Reduced I/O Load audio once per scene, keep in memory Parallelization ready Stage 1 could be parallelized in future Predictable behavior Same processing regardless of scene count

docs.title: Negative
docs.text: Drawback Mitigation Higher peak memory Limit registry size, process in chunks if needed Delayed first output Show progress during Stage 1 to maintain UX Architecture change Keep sequential as fallback, gradual migration Testing complexity Comprehensive test coverage for new data flow

docs.title: Risks
docs.text:
1. Memory exhaustion on very long files (100+ scenes)

  2. Mitigation: Chunk processing with configurable batch size

  3. Regression in edge cases (single scene, no speech detected)

  4. Mitigation: Thorough test coverage, fallback to sequential

  5. Progress UI changes required for two-stage model

  6. Mitigation: Abstract progress interface, update incrementally

docs.title: Phase 1: Foundation (v1.8.0)
docs.text:
• [ ] Implement VADGroupRegistry data structure
  • [ ] Add batch_segment_scenes() to BalancedPipeline
  • [ ] Add transcribe_registry() to FasterWhisperProASR
  • [ ] Update progress reporting for two-stage model
  • [ ] Add feature flag to enable/disable batched processing

docs.title: Phase 2: Optimization (v1.8.x)
docs.text:
• [ ] Implement chunked processing for large files
  • [ ] Add memory profiling and limits
  • [ ] Optimize audio data handling (memory views vs copies)
  • [ ] Performance benchmarking vs sequential

docs.title: Phase 3: Migration (v1.9.0)
docs.text:
• [ ] Make batched processing the default
  • [ ] Deprecate sequential-only code paths
  • [ ] Update documentation and user guides

docs.title: Metrics for Success
docs.text: Metric Target Memory usage < 20% increase in peak usage Processing time No regression (ideally 5-10% improvement) Progress accuracy ETA within 10% of actual for files > 1 minute Test coverage > 90% for new code paths

docs.title: References
docs.text:
• Current implementation: whisperjav/pipelines/balanced_pipeline.py
  • Speech segmentation: whisperjav/modules/speech_segmentation/
  • ASR module: whisperjav/modules/faster_whisper_pro_asr.py
  • Related: ADR-001 (YAML configuration architecture)

docs.title: BalancedPipeline.process() - Scene Loop (lines 278-356)

    scene_srt_path = scene_srts_dir / f"{scene_path.stem}.srt"
    # ... progress update ...

docs.text:

for i, vad_group in enumerate(vad_segments, 1):
